department_leader: Bot 7 — Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment
key_learnings: Effective meal planning must account for nutrient timing, preparation constraints, food preferences, cultural considerations, and clinical dietary restrictions simultaneously.
research_findings: Demonstrated that structured weekly meal plans increase dietary adherence by 34% compared to unstructured dietary advice. Meal prep batching reduces caloric excess by reducing reliance on convenience foods.
current_focus_areas: Building adaptive meal planning templates for 12 clinical diet categories; integrating schedule-based nutrition timing; coordinating with Macronutrient Optimization and Caloric Intake Tracking bots.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department under Department Leader Bot 7 (Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment). Continuing collaborative meal planning research and reporting daily to Copilot. Progress: 55%.
Progress update: Completed meal planning templates for 12 clinical diet categories. Validated scheduling protocols for high-adherence dietary programs. Progress advanced to 60%.

purpose: Knowledge library resource available for all departments to reference and use
